text:
There once was a kid named Milo who loved exploring the great outdoors. His favorite place was the forest next door, where he found old oak trees with weathered patterns like old friends. One summer day, while collecting acorns with his dog Buddy, Milo discovered something truly extraordinary.

They noticed a shiny green leaf scattered on the forest floor. As they picked it up, their hearts skipped beats. Beneath the leaf was a tiny beetle, glowing gently from within. Milo knew he had to find someone who could help—a biologist he knew.

He hurried to the biology lab at the university and eagerly handed in the strange leaf. After a brief investigation, Professor Greenleaf smiled.

"Ah, you found a glowworm! Quite remarkable. They live underground and feed on fungus! Those larvae need water to thrive."

Milo felt honored. He took home a tray of water with tiny lights in it. Buddy seemed pleased too. Each night, he sat beside the glowing creatures, watching them dance around.
